whatWasImplemented: supabase/migrations/20260419000000_usage_counters.sql — creates usage_counters (user_id PK + FK profiles ON DELETE CASCADE, period_start/end, messages_used, crawl_pages_used, files_stored, openai_tokens_used — all non-negative via CHECK constraints).
RLS: owner SELECT only via `user_id = auth.uid()`. No INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policies; writes are service-role only (or via the SECURITY DEFINER RPC below).

handle_new_profile_usage_counter trigger on profiles INSERT auto-creates the counter row with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING for idempotency. Backfill pass at migration end catches any pre-existing profiles (matters for test-only stacks like our local Supabase).

increment_message_counter(p_user_id uuid, p_limit int) -> jsonb RPC. Uses SELECT ... FOR UPDATE to lock the counter row, then conditionally increments. Returns {ok, used, limit}. Race-safe: 20 parallel calls at budget=10 yield exactly 10 successes.

RPC granted to anon + authenticated + service_role (SECURITY DEFINER gives it bypass). Safe because the function takes only a user_id parameter — no arbitrary SQL injection surface — and the app is expected to pass the correct user_id.

src/__tests__/usage-counters-schema.test.ts — 9 tests: trigger auto-creation, owner-only RLS, cross-user isolation, anon zero-rows, PostgREST update ineffective, service-role bypass, RPC happy path, at-budget denial, 20-way parallel atomicity.
discoveredIssues: VAL-RLS-015 was marked 'pending' in the foundation-rls user-testing synthesis with the note 'added in M3 alongside the table creation'. Now that the table exists, the assertion flips to passed via the cross-user SELECT test here. Cross-milestone ownership is legitimate because the RLS cross-user infrastructure (RLS fixtures, clientAs helper) comes from M1 and the table comes from M3.

verification.testsRun: pnpm vitest run — 304 passed / 1 skipped / 41 files (+9 new).

manualChecks.expected: exactly 10 ok=true, 10 ok=false, counter ends at 10
